chapter_context.summary: Continues the prophecy against Moab, advising them to send tribute, expressing compassion for their fugitives, and declaring that Moab's glory will be brought to contempt.

outline.text: Moab Advised to Send Tribute and Shelter Outcasts of Zion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Moab Advised to Send Tribute and Shelter Outcasts of Zion (16:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Lamenting Moab's Pride and Ruin of Her Vineyards
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Lamenting Moab's Pride and Ruin of Her Vineyards (16:6-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Moab's Vain Worship and Swift Decline of Her Glory
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Moab's Vain Worship and Swift Decline of Her Glory (16:12-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 16, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
